question_text: Why do planets orbit the Sun instead of flying off into space?

options.label: A
options.text: The Sun's gravity pulls them towards it

options.label: B
options.text: Magnetic fields hold them in place

options.label: C
options.text: The planets are attached by invisible strings

options.label: D
options.text: They are slowing down and will eventually stop

correct_answer: The Sun's gravity pulls them towards it
explanation: The Sun has a very strong gravitational force that pulls planets towards it. This gravitational pull is balanced by the planets' motion through space, which keeps them moving in stable orbits around the Sun rather than flying off into space or crashing into it.
